rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Final Distribution Of Proceeds Of Sale, For Authority To Make Distribution, For Referee Fees And Costs, Request To Maintain A Closing Reserve, And Request To Be Discharged As Referee
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 22 2025 line 1. OTHER AMY HARRINGTON (PARTITION REFEREE) NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR FINAL DISTRIBUTION OF PROCEEDS OF SALE, FOR AUTHORITY TO MAKE DISTRIBUTION, FOR REFEREE FEES AND COSTS, REQUEST TO MAINTAIN A CLOSING RESERVE, AND REQUEST TO BE DISCHARGED AS REFEREE is GRANTED. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT Q HOANG VS. ANDERSEN, JUNG & CO., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 22 2025 line 2. DEFENDANT MONICA CHUNG AN INDIVIDUAL, EMPRESS REAL ESTATE INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is continued to January 28, 2025 for Plaintiff to file a supplemental brief (not to exceed 2 pages) citing to any cases supporting Plaintiff's position that, he, and not the LLC that purchased the property, is the real party in interest sustaining damages and entitled to recover damages. Civil Code 3342(a). If Plaintiff cannot locate any such cases, Plaintiff shall state "none."
